items.text:
NOTICE: Updated Recreation Center Policies

Effective Monday, September 12, the Hargrove Recreation Center will operate under revised access rules. All students must tap a valid ID card at the front desk before entering the facility. Guest passes are issued only at the main office in Room 105 and require a $5 day fee, payable in cash.

The following changes will take effect this semester:

- The pool will be reserved for swim classes from 7:00 AM to 9:00 AM on weekdays.
- Lockers may be rented for the full term for $20, with a $10 refundable deposit collected at sign-up.
- Equipment, including towels and racquets, must be returned by closing time, which is now 10:00 PM.

Members are reminded that food is not permitted in the weight room, and all personal items left overnight will be donated. Questions about the new policies may be directed to the front desk or emailed to recreation@hargrove.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is required to obtain a guest pass?

options.A: A cash payment at the main office
options.B: A signed form from a member

questions.explanation: Guest passes are issued only at the main office and require a $5 day fee payable in cash, so a cash payment is needed.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who arrive at the pool at 8:00 AM on a weekday?

options.A: They will need to pay an entry fee
options.B: They will be unable to swim freely
options.C: They will receive a locker for free
options.D: They will be asked to leave the center

questions.explanation: The pool is reserved for swim classes from 7:00 AM to 9:00 AM on weekdays, so an 8:00 AM arrival means free swimming is not available.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce the closing of the recreation center
options.B: To inform members of revised facility rules
options.C: To advertise new fitness classes for students
options.D: To request feedback about pool scheduling

questions.explanation: The notice describes several revised access rules and policy changes, so its main purpose is to inform members of the new facility rules.

items.text:
NOTICE: Campus E-Waste Recycling Drive

The Office of Sustainability will host an electronic-waste recycling drive on Saturday, October 21, from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM in South Lot B, behind Carver Hall. Students and faculty are encouraged to dispose of unwanted electronics responsibly rather than placing them in regular trash bins.

Accepted items include:

- Laptops, tablets, and phones
- Cables, chargers, and batteries
- Printers and small monitors

Large appliances and televisions will not be accepted at this event. All hard drives should be erased beforehand, as data security cannot be guaranteed by volunteers. Participants who bring three or more devices will receive a $5 voucher for the campus bookstore.

The drive is staffed entirely by student volunteers from the Green Club. Those wishing to help should sign up online through the sustainability portal by October 18. For further details, contact Ms. Okafor at the Office of Sustainability in Room 318.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where will the recycling drive take place?

options.C: In a parking lot behind Carver Hall

questions.explanation: The drive will be held in South Lot B, which is behind Carver Hall, making it a parking lot behind that building.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must participants do with their hard drives before the event?
questions.explanation: The notice states that all hard drives should be erased beforehand, since data security cannot be guaranteed.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about someone bringing an old television to the event?

options.A: They will earn a bookstore voucher
options.B: They will be turned away with it
options.C: They will need to erase its data
options.D: They will be asked to volunteer

questions.explanation: Since large appliances and televisions will not be accepted, a person bringing a television would have to take it back.
